activities_projects.title: Quantitative Reactivity Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Determine the relative order of reactivity using displacement and thermal data
materials: Magnesium, Zinc, Iron, and Copper strips
steps: Measure the initial temperature of 25ml of $CuSO_4$ solution
Add a fixed mass of Zinc granules and record the maximum temperature reached ($\Delta T$)
Repeat with Magnesium and Iron
Correlate the magnitude of temperature rise with the position of the metal in the reactivity series
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Relates the enthalpy of displacement reactions to the chemical reactivity of metals
activities_projects.evaluation: Data table showing $\Delta T$ values and the resulting reactivity order (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Thermodynamics of Roasting vs. Calcination
activities_projects.objective: Compare the extraction methods for Carbonate and Sulphide ores
steps: Heat Zinc Carbonate in a closed tube (limited air) and pass gas through lime water
Heat Zinc Sulphide in an open tube (excess air) and observe the pungent smell of $SO_2$
Note the color change of the residue (ZnO) when hot and cold
Calculate the theoretical mass loss for each process using stoichiometry
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Distinguishes between calcination and roasting based on the type of ore and atmospheric conditions
activities_projects.evaluation: Balanced chemical equations and identification of the gases evolved in each process (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Self-Reduction Research: The Copper Extraction
activities_projects.objective: Investigate the auto-reduction of Copper Sulphide
steps: Research the reaction between Copper(I) oxide and Copper(I) sulphide
Explain how no external reducing agent like Carbon is required for this specific metal
Draw a detailed flowchart of the extraction of copper from its sulphide ore (Copper Pyrites)
Calculate the percentage of Copper in $Cu_2S$
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Analyzes the unique 'Self-Reduction' mechanism used for less reactive metal ores
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity of the flowchart and accuracy of the self-reduction chemical equation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Electrolytic Extraction of Aluminum (Hall-Héroult)
activities_projects.objective: Understand the role of Cryolite in the electrolysis of Alumina
steps: Study the Hall-Héroult process and why $Al_2O_3$ cannot be reduced by Carbon
Research why Cryolite ($Na_3AlF_6$) is added to the molten Alumina
Explain why the Carbon anodes must be periodically replaced
Calculate the mass of Aluminum produced by the transfer of 3 moles of electrons
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Evaluates the energy-intensive nature of extracting highly reactive metals
activities_projects.evaluation: Explanation of the 'Anode Effect' and the function of Cryolite in lowering the melting point (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Crystal Structure of Ionic Compounds
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the lattice energy and brittle nature of ionic solids
steps: Build a 3D cubic model of $NaCl$ representing $Na^+$ and $Cl^-$ ions
Simulate a 'shear force' by shifting one layer of the model
Observe how like-charges align and cause repulsion (brittleness)
Research the concept of Lattice Enthalpy and its effect on melting points
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Explains the physical properties of ionic compounds through their microscopic lattice arrangement
activities_projects.evaluation: Demonstration of the repulsion effect in the model and definition of lattice energy (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Alloy Design and Properties
activities_projects.objective: Investigate the change in properties of pure metals when alloyed
materials: Samples of Pure Copper, Pure Zinc, and Brass
steps: Measure the electrical conductivity of pure Copper vs. Brass
Test the hardness of each sample using a scratch test or indentation
Research the composition of Solder and why its melting point is lower than its constituents
Explain why 22-carat gold is preferred over 24-carat gold for jewelry
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Concludes how alloying modifies conductivity, hardness, and melting points
activities_projects.evaluation: Comparative table of properties between pure metals and their alloys (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Anodizing Aluminum: The Protective Layer
activities_projects.objective: Mathematically model the growth of the oxide layer
steps: Perform electrolysis with the Aluminum strip as the Anode
Explain the reaction: $2Al + 3O^{2-} ightarrow Al_2O_3 + 6e^-$
Research how this layer can be dyed to produce colored aluminum products
Calculate the theoretical increase in mass of the anode due to the oxide layer
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the industrial application of electrolysis to enhance corrosion resistance
activities_projects.evaluation: Technical description of the anodizing process and its advantages (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Thermite Welding: Stoichiometry and Safety
activities_projects.objective: Calculate the mass of reactants needed for a specific yield of molten Iron
steps: Calculate the mass of Aluminum required to react completely with 1kg of Ferric Oxide
Determine the total heat energy released if the $\Delta H$ is $-851 	ext{ kJ/mol}$
Explain the state of matter of the products during the reaction
Identify the reducing agent and the oxidizing agent in this reaction
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies stoichiometric and thermochemical principles to a real-world engineering reaction
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of the mass-mass calculations and heat energy determination (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Corrosion in Multi-Metal Systems
activities_projects.objective: Observe 'Galvanic Corrosion' when two different metals are in contact
steps: Dish A: Iron nail alone. Dish B: Iron nail wrapped in Copper. Dish C: Iron nail wrapped in Magnesium
Observe the rate of blue color formation (indicating $Fe^{2+}$ ions/rusting)
Analyze why Magnesium protects the iron while Copper accelerates its rusting
Define 'Sacrificial Anode'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Analyzes the electrochemical series to predict which metal will corrode in a bimetallic couple
activities_projects.evaluation: Detailed comparison of the three dishes and the concept of sacrificial protection (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Purity of Gold: Density Method
activities_projects.objective: Use Archimedes' principle to detect impurities in metals
steps: Measure the mass of the sample in air ($m$)
Measure the volume of the sample by water displacement ($V$)
Calculate the density ($ho = m/V$)
Compare the result with the density of pure Gold ($19.3 	ext{ g/cm}^3$) and explain the discrepancy
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies physical constants to verify the purity and authenticity of metallic samples
activities_projects.evaluation: Precision of density calculation and reasoning for impurity detection (10 marks)
